title: schedule.v1
description: The job-spec contract for the runtime scheduler: an HTTP-call request scheduled for future (one-shot or cron) execution, with retry/backoff and idempotency. Derived from 0.11 runtime-api's real job shape.

Request.description: The HTTP call the scheduler fires when the job is due.

body.description: request body (object → JSON, or a raw string)

Retry.description: Exponential-backoff retry policy for a failed dispatch.

backoff.description: per-attempt delay seconds; the last entry is reused past its length

ScheduleJob.description: The schedule() input. Exactly one of execute_at | cron sets the first run; cron also re-arms.

execute_at.description: one-shot fire time — unix epoch seconds (number) or ISO-8601 (string)

cron.description: 5-field cron expression; re-arms after each run

idempotency_key.description: dedup key — a duplicate returns the existing job
